problem_summary: 2023 MCM Problem C (data-driven) on the Wordle Twitter dataset from the NYT. Four tasks: (1a) model how date/other factors drive the daily number of reported results and produce a point + prediction interval for the number of reports on March 1, 2023; (1b) determine whether any attribute of the word itself affects the percentage of scores reported in Hard Mode; (2) predict the full distribution of tries (1/2/3/4/5/6/X, summing to 100%) for a given future date and solution word, with a prediction interval; (3) define and measure word difficulty, cluster words into difficulty groups, characterize each group, and classify new words; (4) report other interesting features of the data. This team (#2307166) earned the Outstanding award.
models_used: PCQL compartmental ODE model — a custom SIR extension with 4 compartments P(otential)/C(rowd)/Q(uitted)/L(oyal) and 4 parameters beta (participation), gamma (boredom), lambda (crowd-to-loyal conversion), phi (loyal boredom); splits the recovered class into Quitted + Loyal to capture habitual players
SIR epidemic model (Kermack-McKendrick) as the conceptual baseline the PCQL model extends

Bi-gram / first-order Markov character-transition model over English 5-letter words (with virtual BOW/EOW tokens) used to engineer two original word features: Regularity (geometric mean of transition probabilities) and Purity (negentropy = negative Shannon entropy of the next-character distribution)
KNN regression to predict the 7-bin tries distribution (chosen specifically because averaging neighbor vectors preserves the sum-to-100% simplex constraint)
KNN classifier to assign words to easy/normal/hard difficulty classes
Genetic algorithm (R 'GA' package) to find a good global initial parameter vector for the ODE fit
Nelder-Mead simplex optimization to refine ODE parameters by minimizing MSE on the numerical ODE solution (fast enough to rerun per bootstrap sample)
Bootstrap (1000 resamples) for prediction intervals on both the ODE forecast and the KNN distribution forecast — percentile method (2.5%/97.5%)
Ordinary least squares linear regression used diagnostically to debunk a spurious correlation (Hard Mode % vs regularity vs time)
key_techniques: Original feature engineering grounded in the game mechanic: Regularity = nth-root product of bigram transition probs P(c_j|c_{j-1}); Purity = -entropy of next-char distribution, motivated by the fact that Wordle feedback reveals letter presence so low-entropy successors are easier to guess
Both frequency-weighted and unweighted versions of each lexical feature, with log-domain computation and zero-frequency clamped to 1 to avoid float underflow

Mechanistic compartment design: redefining S/I/R as target customers / score-sharers / bored players, then arguing from domain mechanism that 'cured' should split into Quitted and Loyal — model structure driven by a real-world story, not curve-fitting alone

Two-stage optimization: GA for a robust global initial solution, then cheap Nelder-Mead per bootstrap replicate — a deliberate speed/robustness trade so bootstrap CIs are computationally feasible on an ODE that has no closed form

Exploiting KNN's averaging property as a constraint-satisfying trick: neighbor-mean of distribution vectors automatically sums to 100%, sidestepping the need for explicit simplex/normalization constraints

Covariance/correlation matrix as a feature pre-screen, then 10-fold cross-validation to jointly choose the feature subset AND K (regression best at K=15 with weighted purity+regularity, excluding Hard-Mode count which is unavailable at prediction time; classifier best at K=8, 75.28% accuracy)

Spurious-regression diagnosis: showing both Hard-Mode% and regularity trend with time (R^2=0.85 on time vs only ~0.02 on each other), concluding the word-attribute correlation is confounded by a common time trend rather than causal
Cross-model corroboration: the empirically rising Hard-Mode report ratio matches the PCQL model's rising Loyal-player fraction, used as independent validation of the latent compartment
Leakage-aware feature selection: explicitly excluding Hard-Mode result count from the tries-distribution model because it is unknown for a future word
Difficulty label construction from data: median number of tries per word, merging the only 3 words with median 6 into the median-5 bucket to form balanced easy/normal/hard classes
why_it_won: "It reframed a data-mining prompt as a mechanism-modeling problem and executed both halves rigorously. The PCQL model is a genuine, well-motivated extension of SIR rather than an off-the-shelf fit, and the team validated it independently by matching the latent Loyal-player curve to the observed Hard-Mode ratio trend — judges reward a model that predicts something it was not fitted to. The two invented lexical features (Regularity and Purity) are creative, information-theoretically principled, and tied directly to how Wordle feedback works, giving the difficulty and distribution models real interpretive substance. The team showed unusual statistical maturity: they caught and explicitly debunked a spurious time-confounded correlation for the Hard-Mode question (most teams would have reported the significant coefficient and stopped), and they were leakage-aware in dropping the unavailable Hard-Mode covariate. Methodologically they paired the right tools — GA+Nelder-Mead for a non-closed-form ODE, bootstrap percentile CIs everywhere an interval was asked, KNN chosen specifically for its simplex-preserving property — and cross-validated both feature sets and hyperparameters. The result is a coherent, honest, end-to-end story with quantified uncertainty on every prediction."
section_highlights: "Section 4 (PCQL model) is the centerpiece: a clean SIR review, an explicit mechanism-driven justification for adding the Loyal compartment, a labeled transition diagram (Figure 5), the full 4-equation ODE system, and a transparent fitting pipeline (GA initial params table -> Nelder-Mead -> 1000 bootstrap -> [11173, 17069] interval). Section 3 (Word Feature Engineering) is the creative core, deriving Regularity and Purity from a bigram chain with BOW/EOW tokens and giving the information-theoretic intuition for why purity maps to guessability. Section 5 is the standout for judging optics: a deliberately structured 'It Seems to Be True' / 'It is Very Likely Not True' pair that walks the reader through a finding and then dismantles it via spurious-regression analysis — a rare display of self-skepticism. Section 6 cleanly motivates KNN via the sum-to-100% constraint and documents the CV grid over feature subsets and K. Section 8 surfaces genuinely interesting cross-model insights (Hard-Mode ratio as a proxy for loyal-player share; repeated-letter words depressing first-try success because opening guesses avoid repeats). The opening one-page memo/Letter to the NYT Puzzle Editor translates every result into plain business language with concrete recommendations."
pitfalls_or_limitations: The OCR'd figures (covariance matrices, CV curves, bar charts in Figures 6-17) are garbled in the markdown and clearly mis-transcribed, so the quantitative tables there are unreliable; some correlation tables show implausibly repeated values (e.g. 0.926*** repeated down a column)

The Purity word-level formula is stated as w_i = sum of character purities but the symbol/definition is sloppy (purity defined with a +log term that should be the negative-entropy; sign/notation is inconsistent between the C-level and word-level definitions)
The bigram/first-order Markov assumption is weak for 5-letter words where non-adjacent letter constraints matter; the model captures only adjacent-pair structure

KNN regression on a small word dataset with K=15 will heavily smooth/regress predictions toward the mean, likely understating tails of the tries distribution; the claimed bootstrap CI of '~5% above and below' may be over-optimistic

Difficulty classes are imbalanced and somewhat arbitrary (median tries 3/4/5-6), and merging the 3 median-6 words into median-5 is a convenience choice; 75.28% accuracy is modest and the [63%,82%] interval is wide

The March 1 2023 report-count forecast assumes player structure is frozen; the team themselves flag that a rising loyal-player fraction could shift future distributions, an admitted unmodeled source of error
Hard-Mode question conclusion ('word attributes do NOT affect Hard-Mode %') rests on the time-confounding argument but does not fully rule out attribute effects within fixed time windows

Reliance on external corpora (bestwordlist.com 5-letter list; hermitdave movie-subtitle frequency list) introduces domain mismatch — subtitle frequency is a proxy for the familiarity that actually drives player guesses
reusable_patterns: When a trend looks like an epidemic/adoption curve (steep rise + long tail, no periodicity), reach for an SIR-family compartment model and customize the compartments to the real mechanism (here: splitting 'recovered' into Quitted vs Loyal)

For any quantity that must lie on a simplex (a distribution summing to 100%), use KNN regression — averaging neighbor vectors preserves the sum constraint for free, avoiding explicit constrained optimization

Engineer domain-specific features from the problem's own mechanic; for text/sequence problems, bigram/Markov transition stats plus information-theoretic measures (entropy/negentropy, geometric-mean transition probability) are cheap, interpretable, and original

Fit non-closed-form ODE models with a two-stage optimizer: genetic algorithm for a robust global starting point, then a fast local method (Nelder-Mead) — and reuse that same start across bootstrap replicates so percentile prediction intervals are tractable
Use bootstrap percentile intervals as a uniform answer to every 'give a prediction interval' sub-question, especially when the estimator has no analytic standard error
Pre-screen features with a correlation/covariance matrix, then let k-fold cross-validation jointly select the feature subset and the hyperparameter (K) — and report the CV curve as evidence

Actively hunt for and debunk spurious correlations: when two variables both trend with time, regress each on time and show the common-trend confound before claiming a relationship — and write it as a deliberate 'seems true / is not true' section to showcase rigor
Guard against data leakage by dropping covariates that won't be available at prediction time (here, future Hard-Mode counts)
Validate a model by checking that a latent/unfitted quantity it implies (loyal-player fraction) matches an independent observed series (Hard-Mode ratio) — free corroboration without extra fitting
Open with a 1-2 page client memo (Letter to the NYT editor) that states every numeric result in plain language with actionable business recommendations, before the technical body
